heading: Regulatory reform
label: 161 Regulatory reform

(a)(1), was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , known as the Communications Act of 1934, which is classified principally to this chapter. children.text: shall review all regulations issued under this chapter in effect at the time of the review that apply to the operations or activities of any provider of telecommunications service; and
shall determine whether any such regulation is no longer necessary in the public interest as the result of meaningful economic competition between providers of such service.

children.heading: Effect of determination
children.label: b Effect of determination
children.text: The Commission shall repeal or modify any regulation it determines to be no longer necessary in the public interest.
